entries.title: CFTC staff details how crypto firms can use digital assets as derivatives collateral in new FAQ
entries.snippet: The FAQ aligns the CFTC's framework with the SEC's recent haircut guidance, setting a 20% charge for bitcoin and ether and 2% for payment stablecoins.
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: CFTC clarifies crypto collateral rules, aligning with SEC haircuts—20% for BTC/ETH, 2% for stablecoins.

This standardizes risk frameworks across regulators, accelerating institutional crypto derivatives adoption.

The payments giant is betting big that stablecoins become essential rails for global commerce.

entries.title: Thunes brings stablecoins to 11,500 banks via existing Swift connections
entries.snippet: Thunes, the Smart Superhighway to move money around the world, today unveils a major breakthrough in cross-border payments by offering its Pay-to-Stablecoin-Wallets solution to banks globally through their existing Swift connectivity.

entries.title: PayPal's stablecoin PYUSD now available in 70 countries instead of just U.S. and U.K.
entries.snippet: CoinDesk (@CoinDesk): JUST IN: @PayPal's stablecoin $PYUSD now available in 70 countries instead of just U.S. and U.K. The expansion enables cross-border transfers and stablecoin rewards in markets where PayPal operates.

entries.title: Vietnam plans to draft new rules banning citizens from trading on overseas crypto platforms like Binance and OKX, while aiming to launch compliant exchanges
entries.snippet: Wu Blockchain (@WuBlockchain): According to Reuters, Vietnam plans to draft new rules banning citizens from trading on overseas crypto platforms like Binance and OKX, while aiming to launch a pilot program for local compliant exchanges as early as this month. A Ministry of Finance document reveals t
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: Vietnam bans citizens from @binance, @okx and overseas crypto platforms, launching domestic compliant exchanges instead.

Watch for regulatory arbitrage shifts as major markets tighten crypto access controls.

entries.title: Sokin hops on stablecoin rails
entries.snippet: Sokin, the global business payments and finance platform, today announced the launch of its stablecoin capabilities, transforming its offering into an all-in-one finance platform that unifies traditional currency treasury operations with stablecoin accounts and settlement rails.
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: Sokin merges traditional treasury with stablecoin settlement in one platform.

This closes the operational gap between legacy banking and crypto rails—watch for enterprise treasury teams to demand similar unified stacks.

entries.title: Mintos introduces crypto ETPs
entries.snippet: Mintos, a European multi-asset investment platform for long-term wealth building, is expanding into crypto by offering regulated exchange-traded products (ETPs) that provide exposure to cryptocurrencies starting from just €5.

entries.title: Vietnam firms vie to launch crypto exchanges ahead of ban on overseas trading

entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: Invesco and DigiFT launch tokenized private credit fund, bringing institutional-grade debt markets on-chain.

This signals traditional asset managers are moving beyond ETFs—expect a wave of illiquid assets tokenization across the industry.

entries.title: OpenSea delays highly anticipated token launch, citing challenging crypto market conditions
T. Rowe Price amends S-1 for actively managed crypto ETF
entries.snippet: New SEC filing names Anchorage Digital Bank as the crypto custodian and adds SUI to the list of eligible tokens in the proposed fund that aims for direct digital asset exposure.

entries.title: SEC's Hester Peirce encourages firms exploring tokenization to 'come in and talk to us'
entries.snippet: Peirce said the SEC isn't a “merit regulator,” emphasizing the agency doesn't decide whether financial products are good or bad investments.

entries.title: SEC Enforcement Division Director Judge Margaret A. Ryan Resigns from Agency
entries.snippet: SEC: SEC Announces Enforcement Division Director Judge Margaret A. Ryan Has Resigned from Agency
entries.tweet_text: What does Judge Margaret Ryan's departure signal about the SEC's regulatory appetite heading into a new administration? Her exit as Enforcement Division Director could reshape how aggressively the agency pursues fintech compliance cases.

entries.title: US, UK, Canada launch joint operation to disrupt crypto fraud
entries.snippet: Dubbed “Operation Atlantic,“ the effort involves law enforcement agencies from the three countries and is aimed at preventing phishing attacks involving cryptocurrencies.

entries.title: Ironlight raises $21 million to expand regulated marketplace for tokenized securities
entries.snippet: Ironlight’s ATS received approval from FINRA last year to support trading in both traditional and tokenized securities.
entries.tweet_text:
Ironlight just secured $21M to scale tokenized securities trading on its FINRA-approved ATS.

The infrastructure for Wall Street's digital future is quietly being built right now.

entries.title: Bithumb, South Korea's second-largest crypto exchange, fined ₩36.8 billion and suspended for AML violations
entries.snippet: Wu Blockchain (@WuBlockchain): Bithumb, South Korea’s second-largest crypto exchange, was fined ₩36.8 billion and received a six-month partial business suspension by the Financial Intelligence Unit for violations of the country’s AML law, involving about 6.65 million compliance breaches.

entries.title: US Secret Service, UK and Canada launch Operation Atlantic targeting crypto approval phishing scams
entries.snippet: The U.S. Secret Service is participating in a multinational operation targeting crypto approval-phishing scams.

entries.title: Reminder: If you still hold USDC bridged via deBridge, please bridge it out as soon as possible
entries.snippet: Zilliqa (@zilliqa): 📢 Reminder: If you still hold USDC bridged via deBridge, please bridge it out as soon as possible. deBridge will sunset on 31 March, 2026 as announced in blog below. Learn more: https://blog.zilliqa.com/strengthening-stablecoin-infrastructure-on-zilliqa-introducing-zusdc-via-xbri

entries.title: South Korea's Hana Group partners with Standard Chartered for digital asset business
entries.snippet: Hana Financial Group and Standard Chartered will explore joint initiatives in digital assets, including stablecoins.
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: South Korea's Hana Financial Group and @StanChart are jointly exploring digital assets and stablecoins—signaling major traditional banks are no longer on the sidelines.

Watch for Asia's first institutionally-backed stablecoin launch.

entries.title: Aave to launch 'Aave Shield' after $50M token swap mishap
entries.snippet: In a post-mortem of the incident, Aave clarified it was not slippage, but an illiquid market that caused the trader to lose over $50 million while swapping USDT for AAVE.
entries.tweet_text:
BREAKING: Aave's $50M token swap disaster wasn't slippage—it was illiquid markets exposing a critical vulnerability in DeFi infrastructure.

The incident reveals why robust risk controls matter as crypto adoption scales toward institutional players.

entries.title: Thailand SEC eyes Travel Rule, tighter crypto ID checks
entries.snippet: TECHINASIA: Thailand SEC eyes Travel Rule, tighter crypto ID checks
entries.tweet_text:
Thailand's SEC is tightening crypto compliance with Travel Rule requirements and stricter ID verification protocols.

This marks a major shift toward AML standards, signaling regulators worldwide will follow suit.

entries.title: Australian Senate committee backs crypto bill requiring platforms to get licensed
entries.snippet: Under the legislation, crypto platforms and tokenized custody platforms would be treated similarly to other financial service providers.
